_README: Depth corrections for the FFIEC 031 Call hierarchy. Root cause: CDR column order != PDF item order; items from one section appear adjacent to items from another, and if the displaced item has PDF depth=2 the audit/visual tree shows it as a false child of the preceding depth=1 item. Fix pattern: drop_codes uses key-scoped {key,mdrm} objects (NOT Y-9C flat strings) because Call codes repeat across 45 schedules — RCFD1773 appears in 5 schedules, RCFD3632 in 3. A flat-string global drop would silently remove correct entries from sibling schedules. drop_code removes the wrong-depth item; force_row re-adds it with corrected depth at the same CDR order position. Consumed by build_hierarchy.py apply_overrides(). Rebuild: python build_hierarchy.py --pdf Call_Cert18409_12312025.PDF

drop_codes._note: RIE: RIAD5523 is Schedule RI-B Part II item 4 (LESS: Write-downs arising from transfers of financial assets), NOT an RI-E item — CDR falsely lists it under RI-E too (form-PDF-verified 2026-07-08, OWNER FINDINGS cycle). Correctly remains in RIBII via its separate row; only the false RIE membership is dropped. Code stays in the display universe via RIBII, so EMPTY_CODES baseline (662) is unaffected.
